artifacts.summary: Official UTM discovery found one verified academic-integrity source about the university anti-plagiarism system. AI-related official pages were event/news or program context and were not used as policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Rencontre UTM-Entreprises : L’UTM à l’ère de l’Intelligence Artificielle
artifacts.queryUsed: Université de Tunis El Manar intelligence artificielle site:utm.rnu.tn OR site:utm.tn

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: L’Université de Tunis El Manar a organisé ... la 4éme journée de recherche "Rencontre UTM- Entreprises : L'UTM à l'ère de l'Intelligence Artificielle"...
artifacts.notes: Useful discovery context for AI activity, but not a policy or guidance source for AI tool use.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official source text supports a narrow candidate claim.
Do not crawl login-gated, CAPTCHA-protected, or robots-disallowed paths.
Do not treat AI event/news pages as binding policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Université de Tunis El Manar states that it makes a similarity-detection platform available to its teacher-researchers for plagiarism prevention and detection, including analysis of digital student submissions to identify uncited similar paragraphs from online texts.
artifacts.normalizedValue: UTM provides a similarity-detection anti-plagiarism platform for teacher-researchers to analyze digital student submissions.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Dans le cadre de sa démarche de mise en place d’outils de prévention et de détection du plagiat, l’Université de Tunis El Manar met à disposition de ses enseignants-chercheurs une plateforme de détection de similitudes. Cette plateforme vous permet d'analyser des travaux rendus par les étudiants sous forme numérique, pour repérer et identifier des paragraphes similaires à des textes disponibles en ligne et dont les sources ne seraient pas citées.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UTM says it provides teacher-researchers with a similarity-detection platform for plagiarism prevention/detection and for analyzing digital student submissions to identify uncited similar paragraphs from online texts.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow academic-integrity candidate. The evidence supports availability and intended use of an anti-plagiarism similarity-detection platform; it does not support a broader AI policy claim.

artifacts.title: Université de Tunis El Manar staged source review
artifacts.summary: The staged bundle records one official UTM academic-integrity source. The supported candidate claim is limited to the university's anti-plagiarism similarity-detection platform for teacher-researchers and digital student submissions. Official AI-related event pages were rejected as event/news-only and were not used as policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No official UTM generative-AI student-use or teaching policy was identified in this crawl; AI-related pages found were event/news or program pages.
The candidate claim should not be generalized beyond the anti-plagiarism platform text on the official source page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
